<think>
We are given the structure of the NetworkPacket and the PacketInfo.
 We are to use the Hexi library to deserialize the packet.

 Steps for deserialize_packet:
 1. Create a hexi::deserializer object from the buffer.
 2. Read the packet_id (uint32_t) using the deserializer's operator>>.
 3. Read the username using the prefixed adaptor (which reads a length-prefixed string).
 4. Read the score count (uint32_t) and then use read_container to read the scores vector.
    However, note that the provided reference material shows that for a vector of uint32_t, we can use the prefixed adaptor which will read the count and then the elements.
    But the specification says: "Read the score count, then use read_container to read the scores vector".
    However, looking at the reference material, the `prefixed` adaptor for a vector will read the count and then the elements. So we can use:
        deserializer >> prefixed(scores);
    This will read the count and then the elements. But note: the specification says to use read_container. However, the provided `prefixed` adaptor for iterable containers does exactly that.

    Alternatively, if we are to follow the specification exactly, we can read the count and then use read_container. But note that the `read_container` function is a private member of the deserializer? Actually, from the reference, it is used by the `prefixed` adaptor. So we can use the adaptor.

    Let's check the reference for `operator>>` for `prefixed<T>`:
        template<is_iterable T>
        deserializer& operator>>(prefixed<T> adaptor) {
            std::uint32_t count = 0;
            *this >> endian::le(count);
            read_container(adaptor.str, count);
            return *this;

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

const uint8_t* get_read_pointer(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    return adaptor.read_ptr();
}

uint8_t* get_write_pointer(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    return adaptor.write_ptr();
}

struct BufferInfo {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo check_buffer_space(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    BufferInfo info;
    info.readable_size = adaptor.size();
    info.free_space = adaptor.free();
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_buffer_data(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    // Create a destination vector with the specified number of bytes
    std::vector<uint8_t> destination(bytes_to_preview);
    
    // Use the copy method to copy data without advancing the read pointer
    buffer.copy(destination.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    
    return destination;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return generate_filled<Size>(value);
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (const auto& element : arr) {
        if (element != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    TypedData result;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    
    adaptor.read(&result.id);
    adaptor.read(&result.value);
    adaptor.read(&result.ratio);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    const auto big_message_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(&big_message_id, sizeof(big_message_id));

    // Convert count to big-endian and write

    const auto big_count = hexi::endian::native_to_big(count);
    stream.write(&big_count, sizeof(big_count));

    // Convert each element in data_values to big-endian and write
    for (const auto& value : packet.data_values) {
        const auto big_value = hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
        stream.write(&big_value, sizeof(big_value));
    }

    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    const auto big_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(&big_checksum, sizeof(big_checksum));

    return buffer;
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<const uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t message_id;
    stream.get(&message_id, 1);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(message_id);

    // Read count and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t count;
    stream.get(&count, 1);
    count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count);

    // Read data_values using read_container and convert each element
    packet.data_values.resize(count);
    stream.read_container(packet.data_values, count);
    for (auto& value : packet.data_values) {
        value = hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
    }

    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t checksum;
    stream.get(&checksum, 1);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);

    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    ProtocolHeader header;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<const uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Use span to get message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    auto message_id_span = stream.span<uint32_t>(1);
    header.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(message_id_span[0]);

    // Skip past the header (already skipped by span)
    // Note: span() method already advances the stream position

    return header;
}
